my_thoughts: Tears—breakthrough. 'I always do that to myself'—they SEE the pattern now. From automatic ('I know that was stupid') to visible ('I always do that'). Five turns from unconscious self-attack to conscious recognition with emotional impact. Teacher notes what's MISSING: 'didn't think of defending yourself.' Self-defense wasn't even option. Just accepted attack as deserved. This plants seed: you CAN defend yourself against internal abuse. But hypothesis: they'll rationalize ('shouldn't I think about mistakes?'). The defense will be 'this is necessary.'
accumulated_insight: T5: Student breaks: '[Tears.] I always do that to myself'—emotional breakthrough. From defending attack (T2) to feeling impact (T4) to breaking (T5). The tears show awareness: I hurt myself habitually. Teacher points out absence: 'You didn't think of defending yourself against this verbal abuse.' Highlights that self-defense wasn't even considered as option. Predicting request_clarification because breakthroughs often lead to 'but what should I do?' questions.
visible_response: ...You didn’t think of defending yourself against this verbal abuse.
